name: Stability-First Family
description: A family system that treats stability as moral correctness and risk as irresponsibility.
life_effect: Non-linear choices require extra justification and are often delayed until success looks almost guaranteed.

name: Achievement-Comparison Family
description: A family system that evaluates love and respect through visible achievement.
life_effect: The person may confuse being admired with being safe.

name: Emotionally Implicit Family
description: A family system where needs are rarely spoken directly and conflict is managed through silence.
life_effect: The person becomes skilled at reading rooms but may struggle to ask directly.

name: Boundary-Entangled Family
description: A family system where personal choices are implicitly evaluated as family loyalty or betrayal.
life_effect: The person may ask whether a choice is allowed before asking whether it is true.

name: Survival-Pragmatic Family
description: A family system shaped by scarcity memory, where emotional dreams must first pass the survival test.
life_effect: The person can become extremely capable, but may postpone softer desires until they feel fully earned.
